Editor identity · the Godot fork

The Workshop

A calm place to make things. This fork wears its own face: warm paper and ink, one muted accent used like a marker in a notebook, and Lato carrying the words. The interface is stage crew — your work is the star.

Typeface Lato (replaces Inter) Default Workshop Paper Accent a single ink-teal Corners quieter · 4→3 Icons calmed · saturation 2.0→1.0

The look, in place

Workshop Paper — the default

Soft off-white paper, dark ink-like text, warm-grey dividers, and one muted accent. Not a bubbly productivity app, not a cold enterprise dashboard — a well-organized desk that doesn't shout.

Workshop Paperbase #EAE7E0 · accent #216B66 · light contrast −0.06

· Saved. Nothing here needs your worry.

Workshop PaperLato · one muted accent · calm by defaultGodot fork · Festival of Disguises

Workshop Ink — after dark

Charcoal and ink, not pure black with neon. The same single accent, lifted just enough to read on a dark ground. Same room, lights low.

· Saved. Nothing here needs your worry.

Workshop InkLato · one muted accent · calm by defaultGodot fork · Festival of Disguises

Both ship in the fork. Editor → Editor Settings → Interface › Theme › Color Preset, or the Project Manager's quick theme picker — the two Workshop presets sit right at the top.

The system

Six colours, one accent at a time

Mostly monochrome, with colour used intentionally. These are the literal values written into the editor's theme generator — hover-free, honest hex, and the exact Color(…) the C++ uses.

Paper
#EAE7E0
Color(0.917, 0.905, 0.878)
Raised
#F6F4EE
panels & fields
Ink
#26231D
primary text
Warm grey
#77715F
secondary & labels
Hairline
#CFC9B9
dividers
Accent
#216B66
Color(0.13, 0.42, 0.40)

Type carries the personality

The app relies on typography instead of clutter. Lato is the human, editorial base — Light for confident titles, Regular for the work, a touch of tracked uppercase for the quiet labels.

DisplayLato Light · −2%
Where am I?
TitleLato Bold
Inspector — Gatekeeper
BodyLato Regular · 14
Everything here has enough room for you to understand it. Readable lists, visible summaries, detail only when it helps.
LabelLato Bold · tracked
FileSystem · Output · Debugger
VoiceCrimson Pro italic
“Saved. Nothing here needs your worry.”

The serif is used only for the console's human voice — a small place where warmth shows through. The chrome stays Lato.

Manifesto → decision

Every choice traces to a line you wrote

“A clean, human sans-serif such as Lato as the base.”

The editor's whole UI font is now Lato, bundled into the engine and set as the built-in default — bold included.

editor_fonts.cpp · thirdparty/fonts/Lato_*

“One muted accent color at a time… like a marker in a notebook, not a carnival.”

A single restrained ink-teal drives every selection, focus ring and active tab. No rainbow of category colours.

accent Color(0.13, 0.42, 0.40)

“Soft off-white backgrounds… dark mode: charcoal and ink, not pure black with neon.”

Workshop Paper (warm paper) is the default; Workshop Ink is a charcoal room, never black-with-neon.

two presets in editor_theme_manager.cpp

“Avoid overuse of rounded cards and pill buttons.”

Corner radius eased from 4 → 3 for a crisper, more editorial edge.

default_corner_radius

“Content is the star; the interface is the stage crew.”

Icon saturation dropped 2.0 → 1.0 so the toolbars read as calm monochrome, not candy.

icon_saturation

“Direct, warm, and a little clever… not cold, not aggressively whimsical.”

Reflected in the demo's own voice — “Nothing here needs your worry.” — the tone this identity invites.

the room, not the wiring

Grounded in the repo

What actually changed

Files touched

  • editor_fonts.cppInter → Lato
  • editor_theme_manager.cpptwo presets
  • editor_theme_manager.hcorners 4→3
  • editor_settings.cppnew defaults
  • thirdparty/fonts/Lato_*.ttf+ OFL licence

Build & wear it

# build the editor for this platform
scons platform=linuxbsd target=editor

# first launch already wears Workshop Paper.
# to switch, open:
Editor › Editor Settings › Interface
      › Theme › Color Preset
   → Workshop Paper / Workshop Ink

The palette, corners and spacing remain fully adjustable — these are new defaults, not locks.

The Workshop · a visual identity for the Godot fork Mostly monochrome. Personality in deliberate flashes.
